metadata.description: Azure region for all resources.
Base name prefix shared across all resource names.
VMSS VM SKU.
Minimum VMSS instance count (also used as initial capacity).
Maximum VMSS instance count.
Enable TLS syslog listener (RFC 5425 port 6514).
TLS syslog port.
Enable plain-text syslog listener (port 514 TCP + UDP).
Plain-text syslog port.
Allowed source CIDRs for all syslog NSG rules.
True = public Standard LB with a PIP. False = internal LB, no PIP.
Create a new Log Analytics workspace.
Name of the Log Analytics workspace (new or existing).
Full resource ID of an existing workspace. Required only when createWorkspace = false.
Onboard Microsoft Sentinel on the workspace.
VNet address space.
Subnet address prefix (must fall within vnetAddressPrefix).
Resource tags applied to every resource.
Admin username for VMSS instances.
SSH public key for the admin user (not a secret — public material).
PEM-encoded CA certificate. When non-empty, injected into Key Vault at deploy time (single-shot TLS deploy). Empty = manual upload.
PEM-encoded server certificate. When non-empty, injected into Key Vault at deploy time. Empty = manual upload.
PEM-encoded server private key. When non-empty, injected into Key Vault at deploy time. Empty = manual upload.
Base name prefix for all resources.
Azure region.
Resource tags.
Full resource ID of the UAMI.
Principal (object) ID of the UAMI — used for RBAC assignments.
Client ID of the UAMI — used in AMA extension settings.
Base name prefix for all resources.
Azure region.
VNet address space CIDR.
Subnet address prefix CIDR.
Source CIDRs allowed to send syslog. Applied to every syslog NSG rule.
Enable TLS syslog listener rule (port tlsPort).
TLS syslog port (default 6514 per RFC 5425).
Enable plaintext syslog listener rules (port plaintextPort).
Plaintext syslog port (default 514).
Resource tags.

variables.tlsRule: [if(parameters('enableTls'), createArray(createObject('name', 'Allow-Syslog-TLS', 'properties', createObject('description', 'Allow TLS syslog ingestion (RFC 5425) from authorised sources.', 'priority', 100, 'direction', 'Inbound', 'access', 'Allow', 'protocol', 'Tcp', 'sourceAddressPrefixes', parameters('allowedSyslogSourceCidrs'), 'sourcePortRange', '*', 'destinationAddressPrefix', '*', 'destinationPortRange', string(parameters('tlsPort'))))), createArray())]
variables.plaintextRule: [if(parameters('enablePlaintext'), createArray(createObject('name', 'Allow-Syslog-Plain-TCP', 'properties', createObject('description', 'Allow plain-text TCP syslog from authorised sources (legacy fallback).', 'priority', 200, 'direction', 'Inbound', 'access', 'Allow', 'protocol', 'Tcp', 'sourceAddressPrefixes', parameters('allowedSyslogSourceCidrs'), 'sourcePortRange', '*', 'destinationAddressPrefix', '*', 'destinationPortRange', string(parameters('plaintextPort')))), createObject('name', 'Allow-Syslog-Plain-UDP', 'properties', createObject('description', 'Allow plain-text UDP syslog from authorised sources (legacy fallback).', 'priority', 210, 'direction', 'Inbound', 'access', 'Allow', 'protocol', 'Udp', 'sourceAddressPrefixes', parameters('allowedSyslogSourceCidrs'), 'sourcePortRange', '*', 'destinationAddressPrefix', '*', 'destinationPortRange', string(parameters('plaintextPort'))))), createArray())]

properties.description: Explicit deny for all other inbound traffic.

metadata.description: Resource ID of the default subnet.
Resource ID of the NSG.
Resource ID of the VNet.
NAT Gateway outbound public IP — the source IP all VMSS instances appear as for egress.
Azure region.
When true, a new Log Analytics workspace is created. When false, an existing workspace is referenced via existingWorkspaceId.
Name of the new workspace. Also used to derive the Sentinel solution name.
Full resource ID of an existing Log Analytics workspace. Required when createWorkspace = false.
When true, Microsoft Sentinel (SecurityInsights) is onboarded on the workspace.
Resource tags.
Resource ID of the Log Analytics workspace (new or existing).
Name of the Log Analytics workspace.
Base name prefix for all resources.
Azure region.
When true, a Standard public IP is created and attached to the LB frontend. When false, an internal frontend is used (private IP from the subnet).
Subnet resource ID — required for internal LB frontend.
Enable TLS syslog LB rule (TCP tlsPort).
TLS syslog port.
Enable plaintext syslog LB rules (TCP + UDP plaintextPort).
Plaintext syslog port.
Resource tags.

variables.tlsLbRule: [if(parameters('enableTls'), createArray(createObject('name', 'rule-syslog-tls', 'properties', createObject('protocol', 'Tcp', 'frontendPort', parameters('tlsPort'), 'backendPort', parameters('tlsPort'), 'enableFloatingIP', false(), 'idleTimeoutInMinutes', 4, 'frontendIPConfiguration', createObject('id', resourceId('Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers/frontendIPConfigurations', variables('lbName'), variables('frontendName'))), 'backendAddressPool', createObject('id', resourceId('Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers/backendAddressPools', variables('lbName'), variables('backendPoolName'))), 'probe', createObject('id', resourceId('Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers/probes', variables('lbName'), variables('probeName')))))), createArray())]
variables.plaintextTcpLbRule: [if(parameters('enablePlaintext'), createArray(createObject('name', 'rule-syslog-plain-tcp', 'properties', createObject('protocol', 'Tcp', 'frontendPort', parameters('plaintextPort'), 'backendPort', parameters('plaintextPort'), 'enableFloatingIP', false(), 'idleTimeoutInMinutes', 4, 'frontendIPConfiguration', createObject('id', resourceId('Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers/frontendIPConfigurations', variables('lbName'), variables('frontendName'))), 'backendAddressPool', createObject('id', resourceId('Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers/backendAddressPools', variables('lbName'), variables('backendPoolName'))), 'probe', createObject('id', resourceId('Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers/probes', variables('lbName'), variables('probeName')))))), createArray())]
variables.plaintextUdpLbRule: [if(parameters('enablePlaintext'), createArray(createObject('name', 'rule-syslog-plain-udp', 'properties', createObject('protocol', 'Udp', 'frontendPort', parameters('plaintextPort'), 'backendPort', parameters('plaintextPort'), 'enableFloatingIP', false(), 'idleTimeoutInMinutes', 4, 'frontendIPConfiguration', createObject('id', resourceId('Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers/frontendIPConfigurations', variables('lbName'), variables('frontendName'))), 'backendAddressPool', createObject('id', resourceId('Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers/backendAddressPools', variables('lbName'), variables('backendPoolName'))), 'probe', createObject('id', resourceId('Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers/probes', variables('lbName'), variables('probeName')))))), createArray())]
variables.probes: [if(or(parameters('enableTls'), parameters('enablePlaintext')), createArray(createObject('name', variables('probeName'), 'properties', createObject('protocol', 'Tcp', 'port', variables('probePort'), 'intervalInSeconds', 15, 'numberOfProbes', 2))), createArray())]

frontendIPConfigurations.name: [variables('frontendName')]
frontendIPConfigurations.properties: [if(parameters('loadBalancerPublic'), createObject('publicIPAddress', createObject('id', resourceId('Microsoft.Network/publicIPAddresses', format('{0}-pip', parameters('baseName'))))), createObject('subnet', createObject('id', parameters('subnetId')), 'privateIPAllocationMethod', 'Dynamic'))]

metadata.description: Resource ID of the load balancer.
Resource ID of the backend address pool.
Name of the load balancer (used by VMSS IP config reference).
Public IP address (empty string when loadBalancerPublic = false).
Naming prefix for resources, e.g. sc-syslog.
Azure region where the Key Vault will be created.
Object ID (principalId) of the AMA / VMSS User-Assigned Managed Identity.
AAD tenant ID. Defaults to the current subscription tenant.
PEM-encoded CA certificate. When non-empty, created as KV secret syslog-ca-cert at deploy time (single-shot deploy path). Empty = manual-upload path (backward compatible).
PEM-encoded server certificate. When non-empty, created as KV secret syslog-server-cert at deploy time. Empty = manual-upload path.
PEM-encoded server private key. When non-empty, created as KV secret syslog-server-key at deploy time. Empty = manual-upload path.

properties.description: AMA UAMI — read syslog TLS secrets

metadata.description: Base name prefix for all resources.
Azure region.
VM SKU for VMSS instances.
Initial (minimum) instance count.
Resource ID of the subnet to attach VMSS NICs to.
Resource ID of the LB backend address pool.
Resource ID of the User-Assigned Managed Identity (for AMA).
Client ID of the User-Assigned Managed Identity — injected into cloud-init for IMDS cert fetch.
Key Vault name (e.g. kv-sc-syslog-abc12345) — injected into cloud-init for IMDS cert fetch.
Admin username for the Linux OS profile.
SSH public key for the admin user. Not a secret — public key material.
Resource tags.

variables.$fxv#0:
#cloud-config
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# cloud-init for Syslog-VMSS-AMA collector
# Replaces the upstream runtime download of Forwarder_AMA_installer.py
# (deviation-log item 5).
#
# What this does:
#   1. Installs rsyslog + rsyslog-gnutls
#   2. Writes /etc/rsyslog.d/50-tls-listener.conf (TLS config, port 6514)
#   3. Writes /usr/local/bin/fetch-kv-certs.sh (IMDS cert-fetch script)
#   4. Creates /etc/rsyslog.d/certs/ with secure permissions
#   5. Fetches cert material from Key Vault via IMDS managed-identity token
#   6. Restarts rsyslog
#
# ── Certificate Delivery (SINGLE MECHANISM) ──────────────────────────────────
#
# The KeyVaultForLinux VM extension is NOT used — it writes opaque filenames
# that conflict with the canonical names rsyslog expects.  The IMDS fetch
# below is the ONLY cert delivery path.
#
# KV_NAME and UAMI_CLIENT_ID are templated into this file by Bicep at compile
# time (bicep/modules/vmss.bicep uses loadTextContent + replace + base64).
# Placeholder tokens __KV_NAME__ and __UAMI_CLIENT_ID__ are replaced with the
# actual Key Vault name and UAMI client ID derived from the deployed resources.
#
# Secret names in Key Vault → canonical file paths on disk:
#   syslog-ca-cert     → /etc/rsyslog.d/certs/ca.pem         (0644)
#   syslog-server-cert → /etc/rsyslog.d/certs/server.pem     (0644)
#   syslog-server-key  → /etc/rsyslog.d/certs/server-key.pem (0600)
#
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

package_update: true
package_upgrade: false

packages:
  - rsyslog
  - rsyslog-gnutls
  # python3 and curl are pre-installed on Ubuntu 22.04; listed for clarity.
  # azure-cli is NOT required — the fetch script uses raw IMDS + curl only.

write_files:
  # ── rsyslog TLS listener config ────────────────────────────────────────────
  - path: /etc/rsyslog.d/50-tls-listener.conf
    owner: root:root
    permissions: '0644'
    content: |
      # rsyslog TLS listener — RFC 5425 port 6514, GnuTLS driver
      # Canonical source: bicep/scripts/rsyslog-tls.conf
      # See docs/tls-setup.md for cert generation and rotation instructions.
      #
      # AuthMode:
      #   "anon"      — TLS encryption only, no client cert required (DEFAULT)
      #   "x509/name" — mutual TLS; change AuthMode and uncomment PermittedPeers
      module(load="imtcp"
             StreamDriver.Name="gtls"
             StreamDriver.Mode="1"
             StreamDriver.AuthMode="anon")
      # mTLS: change above to AuthMode="x509/name" and uncomment:
      # $ActionSendStreamDriverPermittedPeer *.yourdomain.example.com

      global(
        DefaultNetstreamDriver="gtls"
        DefaultNetstreamDriverCAFile="/etc/rsyslog.d/certs/ca.pem"
        DefaultNetstreamDriverCertFile="/etc/rsyslog.d/certs/server.pem"
        DefaultNetstreamDriverKeyFile="/etc/rsyslog.d/certs/server-key.pem"
      )

      # RFC 5425 TLS listener
      input(type="imtcp" port="6514" name="tls-syslog")

      # Events flow to the systemd journal automatically (Ubuntu 22.04 default).
      # AMA reads from the journal — no additional forward action needed.

      # OPTIONAL plaintext UDP fallback — DISABLED
      # Uncomment only for legacy senders; keep NSG source locked down.
      # module(load="imudp")
      # input(type="imudp" port="514" name="plain-udp")

  # ── IMDS cert-fetch script ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  - path: /usr/local/bin/fetch-kv-certs.sh
    owner: root:root
    permissions: '0700'
    content: |
      #!/usr/bin/env bash
      # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      # fetch-kv-certs.sh — Retrieve syslog TLS certs from Azure Key Vault
      # using the VM's User-Assigned Managed Identity via IMDS.
      #
      # KV_NAME and UAMI_CLIENT_ID are baked in by Bicep at deploy time via
      # loadTextContent + replace + base64 in bicep/modules/vmss.bicep.
      # Placeholders __KV_NAME__ and __UAMI_CLIENT_ID__ are substituted before
      # this file reaches the VM — the values below are final resolved values.
      # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      set -euo pipefail

      KV_NAME="__KV_NAME__"
      UAMI_CLIENT_ID="__UAMI_CLIENT_ID__"

      CERT_DIR="/etc/rsyslog.d/certs"
      mkdir -p "$CERT_DIR"

      # Acquire OAuth token from IMDS for Key Vault audience
      TOKEN=$(curl -sf \
        -H "Metadata: true" \
        "http://169.254.169.254/metadata/identity/oauth2/token?api-version=2018-02-01&resource=https%3A%2F%2Fvault.azure.net&client_id=${UAMI_CLIENT_ID}" \
        | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['access_token'])")

      KV_URI="https://${KV_NAME}.vault.azure.net/secrets"

      fetch_secret() {
        local secret_name="$1" dest_path="$2"
        curl -sf \
          -H "Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" \
          "${KV_URI}/${secret_name}?api-version=7.4" \
          | python3 -c "import sys,json; sys.stdout.write(json.load(sys.stdin)['value'])" \
          > "${dest_path}"
        echo "  Wrote ${dest_path}"
      }

      echo "Fetching TLS cert material from Key Vault: ${KV_NAME}"
      # Secret names → canonical file paths (must match rsyslog-tls.conf references)
      fetch_secret "syslog-ca-cert"     "${CERT_DIR}/ca.pem"
      fetch_secret "syslog-server-cert" "${CERT_DIR}/server.pem"
      fetch_secret "syslog-server-key"  "${CERT_DIR}/server-key.pem"

      # Lock down private key; certs world-readable by root
      chmod 600  "${CERT_DIR}/server-key.pem"
      chmod 644  "${CERT_DIR}/ca.pem" "${CERT_DIR}/server.pem"
      chown -R root:root "${CERT_DIR}"

      echo "Certificate fetch complete."

runcmd:
  # ── 1. Create cert directory ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  - mkdir -p /etc/rsyslog.d/certs
  - chmod 700 /etc/rsyslog.d/certs
  - chown root:root /etc/rsyslog.d/certs

  # ── 2. Fetch TLS cert material from Key Vault via IMDS ───────────────────
  # KV_NAME and UAMI_CLIENT_ID were templated into fetch-kv-certs.sh by Bicep.
  - /usr/local/bin/fetch-kv-certs.sh

  # ── 3. Validate cert files exist before rsyslog starts ───────────────────
  - |
    MISSING=0
    for f in /etc/rsyslog.d/certs/ca.pem \
              /etc/rsyslog.d/certs/server.pem \
              /etc/rsyslog.d/certs/server-key.pem; do
      if [ ! -f "$f" ]; then
        echo "WARNING: ${f} not found — rsyslog TLS will not start." >&2
        MISSING=1
      fi
    done
    # Do not hard-fail here; let rsyslog report the config error clearly.

  # ── 4. Enforce cert directory permissions ────────────────────────────────
  - chmod 600 /etc/rsyslog.d/certs/server-key.pem 2>/dev/null || true
  - chmod 644 /etc/rsyslog.d/certs/ca.pem /etc/rsyslog.d/certs/server.pem 2>/dev/null || true
  - chown -R root:root /etc/rsyslog.d/certs

  # ── 5. Validate rsyslog config syntax, then enable and start ─────────────
  - rsyslogd -N1
  - systemctl enable rsyslog
  - systemctl restart rsyslog

metadata.description: Resource ID of the VMSS.
